title: Neural Network Tearing and Interconnecting Methods for PDEs
abstract: A neural-network analogue of tearing-and-interconnecting domain decomposition is developed for elliptic PDEs, with independently trained subdomain solvers, iterative interface updates, preconditioning, parallelism, and convergence analysis.
notes: SISC 48(3), C553-C578; published online 2026-06-17. Highly relevant to modular subdomain and multiphysics coupling.

title: Multiscale Neural Networks for Approximating Green’s Functions
abstract: Multiscale neural networks are used to approximate low-regularity Green’s functions, with Barron-space analysis showing reduced network size and faster training for fixed-operator PDE solvers.

title: Rank-Inspired Neural Network for Solving Partial Differential Equations
abstract: RINN adds a covariance-driven orthogonalization and preconditioning stage to physics-informed extreme learning machines, reducing initialization sensitivity before least-squares enforcement of PDE constraints.
notes: SISC 48(3), C453-C478; code and data reproducibility badge.

title: Active operator learning with predictive uncertainty quantification for partial differential equations
abstract: A lightweight predictive-UQ framework for DeepONet and FNO supports calibrated out-of-distribution error estimates, faster inference, Bayesian optimization, and active learning in function spaces.
notes: JCP 555, article 114791; formal match to arXiv:2503.03178.

title: A flow-aware training strategy for physics-informed neural networks
venue: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering
abstract: The method partitions the domain into geodesic subdomains and progressively activates PINN supervision according to physical information flow, avoiding misleading gradients in causally unreachable regions.

title: A machine learning method for solving PDEs with a robust initial learning rate based on Lagrange multiplier optimization
abstract: A Lagrange-multiplier optimization formulation builds on scalar-auxiliary-variable energy descent to obtain robust large initial learning rates for neural PDE solvers.

title: Convolution-weighting method for the physics-informed neural network: A primal-dual optimization perspective
abstract: CWP-PINN replaces isolated pointwise residual weighting with spatially coherent convolutional neighborhood weights derived from a primal-dual optimization view.
notes: JCP 555, article 114773; formal match to arXiv:2506.19805.

title: diffSPH: Differentiable smoothed particle hydrodynamics for hybrid machine learning solutions in fluid mechanics
abstract: An open-source PyTorch differentiable SPH framework supports compressible, weakly compressible, and incompressible flows, inverse problems, optimization, and solver-network hybridization.
notes: JCP 555, article 114769; relevant to moving interfaces, multiphase flow, differentiable simulation, and solver-in-the-loop learning.

title: Hybrid deep learning and iterative methods for accelerated solutions of viscous incompressible flow
abstract: HyDEA combines DeepONet-predicted line-search directions with CG/PCG refinement for pressure-Poisson solves, retaining iterative robustness while improving cross-geometry and cross-resolution acceleration.
notes: JCP 554, article 114747; strong reference for neural preconditioners and hybrid solver design.

title: HyMeshAI: Deep learning enabled three-dimensional adaptive mesh generator for high-resolution atmospheric simulations
institutions: Institute of Atmospheric Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences
abstract: HyMeshAI combines CNN-based mesh-density prediction and ANN-based nodal positioning for end-to-end dynamic three-dimensional AMR mesh generation under irregular geometry and variable mesh size.
notes: JCP 554, article 114760; directly relevant to learning-assisted AMR and FLASH-like mesh workflows.

title: Noise-robust multi-fidelity surrogate modelling for parametric partial differential equations
abstract: A noise-robust multi-index stochastic collocation surrogate is developed for parametric PDE quantities of interest when low-fidelity evaluations contain discretization, tolerance, or transient noise.
notes: JCP 554, article 114761; includes advection-diffusion and turbulent incompressible Navier-Stokes tests.

title: A review of machine learning-driven studies of tearing modes in tokamaks
institutions: MIT Plasma Science and Fusion Center
abstract: A review of machine-learning methods for interpreting, predicting, and controlling tearing-mode onset in tokamaks, emphasizing physics-aware validation and multi-machine databases.
notes: Physics of Plasmas 33, 050501; direct plasma-ML relevance but not an original solver method.
